coverage.raw_xml_dropped_reason: Cobertura XML was 11 MB. Per-file summary in coverage.json is enough; raw XML can be regenerated with 'dotnet run --project PLang.Tests --no-build -- --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura'.

findings.issue: All 8 BuildingGuard tests fail. Tests assert Building.IsEnabled guard returns 'Building is not enabled' error. The guard existed in runtime2 baseline (BuildingGuard helper + 8 call sites) and was deleted by the v2 builder squash 50351d8b. The test file is byte-identical to runtime2; the production code lost the implementation.
findings.impact: Builder actions (goals, GetActions, validate, goalsSave, app, appSave, merge, types) execute regardless of Building flag. Mode safety is gone. Codeanalyzer's 4 rounds never ran the tests, so this slipped through.
findings.suggestion: Restore BuildingGuard helper in DefaultBuilderProvider and call from each action method, OR delete the tests with explicit justification (e.g. guard moved). I found no replacement layer.

findings.issue: Test fails with ~80+ conversion errors of the form: 'Cannot convert int = 1 (String) to Int32'. The @known annotation strings (literal 'int = 1', 'bool = false', 'String', 'Dictionary`2') are flowing through Convert.ChangeType instead of being unwrapped first. Affects almost every action's parameter coercion path.
findings.impact: Build-time validation cannot validate parameter shapes when @known annotations are present. The unwrap step that should turn 'int = 1' into 1 is missing or skipped.
findings.suggestion: Find the @known unwrap path; trace why the annotation strings reach Convert.ChangeType. Likely interacts with the locale fix at TypeConverter:325 (both code paths share the same conversion entry).

findings.issue: Loop test expects sum=3, gets concatenation '0 + 1 + 1 + 1'. The arithmetic add operation is being treated as string concatenation. Multiple Loop / Foreach / Condition tests fail.
findings.impact: Foreach with numeric accumulator does not produce the intended numeric sum. Real production regression.

findings.issue: 9 signing tests fail with mixed errors: 'Contract mismatch', 'File not found: .build/sign.pr', 'Action timeout.after.after not found'. The last is at minimum a module/action routing bug — extra '.after' segment.
findings.impact: Signing pipeline is broken end-to-end across multiple invariants.

findings.issue: v4 carryover. TypeConverter.cs:325 parses with InvariantCulture, but the three FORMAT sites use Thread.CurrentCulture. No test sets a non-Invariant culture and asserts numeric round-trip. App.Culture property defaults to Invariant but is never read by any production code (verified by grep across PLang/App).
findings.impact: European-locale users (it-IT, es-ES, de-DE, etc.) building goals with numeric @known values produce '3,14' formals the parser cannot read back. The fix is asymmetric — worse than no fix on those locales because before the fix both ends used current culture and round-tripped consistently.
findings.suggestion: Wire App.Culture into the three format sites OR pass CultureInfo.InvariantCulture directly. Add a test that sets Thread.CurrentCulture to it-IT, calls FormatFormalValue(3.14), and asserts the result is '3.14'.

findings.issue: v4 carryover. Both files have 0% line coverage. promoteGroups is registered as an Action but no goal calls it. enrichResponse is referenced by os/system/builder/ApplyStep.goal but only at build time — the test suite never exercises that path. The new ActionError('PromoteGroupsImmutableStep') has zero verification.
findings.impact: Both code paths can regress silently. enrichResponse is on the hot path of every build but no test covers its current behavior, so any regression to the build output goes uncaught.
findings.suggestion: Add a unit test or a PLang .test.goal that hits each at least once. Or delete promoteGroups (unreachable from any current goal).

findings.issue: Coder Gap 2 (file.read ResolveVariables) — handover listed 3 test cases. Zero tests written. Grep across PLang.Tests, Tests, tests, os: no occurrences of 'ResolveVariables' or 'load vars' in any .cs or .goal test. file/read.cs coverage is 62.5%; the uncovered range is exactly the resolve-vars branch.
findings.impact: The new feature ships untested. Any regression to Variables.Resolve(content) goes uncaught.
findings.suggestion: Add three test cases: (a) ResolveVariables=false returns raw content with unresolved %var%, (b) ResolveVariables=true with %name% in content returns resolved value, (c) ResolveVariables=true with no variables returns content unchanged.

findings.issue: Coder Gap 3 (TypeMapping single→list auto-wrap). Implementation lives at TypeConverter.cs:156-168 (the listElementType.IsAssignableFrom branch and the convert-then-wrap fallback). TypeMappingTests.cs has six TryConvertTo tests; none exercise List<T> targets. Coverage on TypeConverter.cs is 50.4%.
findings.impact: Regression to the wrap logic ships silent. Originally motivated by goalsSave taking List<Goal>; coder later changed it to single Goal, so the production trigger is reduced — but other callers (LLM-emitted lists with 1 element vs scalar) still depend on this path.
findings.suggestion: Add tests for: (a) single Goal → List<Goal>, (b) existing list → no double-wrap, (c) string → List<string>, (d) JsonElement-array → List<T> (production code at TypeConverter:114-124 is also untested).

findings.issue: Handle_RetryFirst_PersistentFailure_AllRetriesFail and Handle_GoalFirst_NoGoal_ExhaustsRetriesAndFails check only Success.IsFalse() (no retry-count verification). Deletion test: if the retry loop body were 'for (int i = 0; i < 0; i++)' (never retried), error.throw still fails on first attempt and the assertion still passes.
findings.impact: A silent regression that disables retries entirely would not fail these tests.
findings.suggestion: Use a counter-based action (variable.add on each retry) and assert the counter equals retryCount + 1, OR inspect Error.ErrorChain length to count retry attempts.

findings.issue: If_OrchestratedBranchAction_ReturnsError_PropagatesThroughStep checks Error.IsNotNull but not the identity. The intent is 'goal.call 404 must surface'. Any unrelated error of the chosen branch would still pass the assertion.
findings.impact: A regression that swapped the surfaced error for a misleading one (e.g. 'condition evaluated false' from condition.if's own validation) would not fail the test.

findings.issue: NullInputs_ReturnsError checks Error.Key == 'ValidationError' but no Message. validateResponse emits multiple distinct ValidationError variants (null inputs, step-count, gap, Keep-without-prior). All share the same Key, so Key alone cannot distinguish them.
findings.impact: A regression that surfaces a different ValidationError from this code path would not fail the test.
findings.suggestion: Assert on a discriminating substring of Message — e.g. 'StepResults' or 'is null'.

findings.issue: Six call sites use Assert.That(result.Success).IsFalse() with no following Error.Key check. Each is a missed opportunity to pin the failure mode.
findings.impact: Lower than F5/F6 but the same pattern. A regression that swaps the error type ships silent.
findings.suggestion: Treat 'Success.IsFalse without Error.Key' as a code-review gate going forward.

summary: The test suite is doing its job. 34 honest red tests surface a production regression (BuildingGuard, missed by 4 codeanalyzer rounds), a builder type-conversion bug ('int = 1' strings reaching Convert.ChangeType), and clusters in Loop/Signing/Identity. Both v4 carryovers confirmed: locale-format asymmetry has zero coverage, promoteGroups + enrichResponse have 0% coverage. Three coder-handover gaps (AsDefault, ResolveVariables, list auto-wrap) ship with under-tested or missing direct unit tests. Recommend sending back to coder.
